cooked:
Yes, with this thread, Im officially starting The LBA 2 Engine Project.

Goal? To reverse-engineer LBA 2 back to C/C++.

Heres whats already done:
- Main menu [Incomplete];
- Body models (thanks to yaz0r).

Anyway, heres the main menu part (incomplete for now):

void InitializeMainMenu();
// NOTE: Setting the X and Y coordinate of the buttons is not yet implemented.
int Idx;
string Text;
{
    Idx = 0;
    if (game.InGame == true)
    {
        Idx = 1;
    }
    //InText(Text Data Index (*2 = Text File, (*2)+1=Text Index Table), Text Index)
    Text = InText({Menu Text Index}, {"Load game" index});
    mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].Caption = Text;
    Idx++;
    Text = InText({Menu Text Index}, {"Save game" index});
    mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].Caption = Text;
    if (game.InGame == false)
    {
        Idx++;
    }
    Text = InText({Menu Text Index}, {"Options" index});
    mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].Caption = Text;
    Idx++;
    Text = InText{Menu Text Index}, {"Quit" index});
    mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].Caption = Text;
    if (game.InGame == true)
    {
        Idx = 0;
        Text = InText({Menu Text Index}, {"Return to game" index});
        mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].Caption = Text;
    }
    if (game.InGame == true)
    {
        mainMenu.ItemsCount = 4;
    }
    else
    {
        mainMenu.ItemsCount = 6;
    }
}

void DrawMainMenu();
{
    DrawImage(LoadHQR("SCREEN.HQR", MenuBackgroundIndex));
    for (int i = 1; i <= mainMenu.ItemsCount; i++)
    {
        DrawMenuButton(mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].Caption, mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].X, mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].Y);
        if (mainMenu.SelectedMenuItem = idx)
        {
            DrawMenuButtonHighlight(mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].Caption, mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].X, mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].Y);
        }
    }
}

void LoadMainMenu();
{
    int ReadStr;

    InitializeMainMenu;
    mainMenu.SelectedMenuItem = 0;
    DrawMainMenu;
    while (mainMenu.MenuString != "\027")
        ReadStr = BackgroundCharRead;
        mainMenu.MenuString += ReadStr;
        // NOTE: The correct reading of the string has yet to be implemented,
        // but I unfortunately don't know, how to get the rightmost n characters
        // out of a string in C.
        if (mainMenu.MenuString.ToUppercase == "LIFE")
        {
            twinsen.Life = maxLife;
            mainMenu.MenuString = "";
        }
        elseif (mainMenu.MenuString.ToUppercase == "MAGIC")
        {
            twinsen.Magic = maxMagic[twinsen.magicLevel];
            mainMenu.MenuString = "";
        }
        elseif (mainMenu.MenuString.ToUppercase =="GOLD")
        {
            // NOTE: This part can be reduced, what will be done later.
            if (game.Planet > 0)
            {
                if (twinsen.Zlitos < 999)
                {
                    if (twinsen.Zlitos < 950)
                    {
                        twinsen.Zlitos += 50;
                    }
                    else
                    {
                        twinsen.Zlitos = 999;
                    }
                }
            }
            else
            {
                if (twinsen.Kashes < 999)
                {
                    if (twinsen.Kashes < 950)
                    {
                        twinsen.Kashes += 50;
                    }
                    else
                    {
                        twinsen.Kashes = 999;
                    }
                }
            }
            mainMenu.MenuString = "";
        }
        elseif (mainMenu.MenuString.ToUppercase == "PINGOUIN")
        {
            if (twinsen.InventoryItems[Pingouin].Count < 10)
            {
                twinsen.InventoryItems[Pingouin].Count += 5;
            }
            mainMenu.MenuString = "";
        }
        elseif (mainMenu.MenuString.ToUppercase == "CLOVER")
        {
            for (int i; i <= twinsen.BoxCount; i++)
            {
                twinsen.BoxFull[i] = true;
            }
            mainMenu.MenuString = "";
        }
        elseif (mainMenu.MenuString.ToUppercase == "BOX")
        {
            if (twinsen.BoxCount < 10)
            {
                twinsen.BoxCount++;
                for (int i; i <= twinsen.BoxCount; i++)
                {
                    twinsen.BoxFull[i] = true;
                }
            }
            mainMenu.MenuString = "";
        }
        elseif (mainMenu.MenuString.ToUppercase == "FULL")
        {
            twinsen.Life = maxLife;
            twinsen.InventoryItems[TritonHorn].Life = maxHornLife;
            twinsen.Magic = maxMagic[twinsen.magicLevel];
            for (int i; i <= twinsen.BoxCount; i++)
            {
                twinsen.BoxFull[i] = true;
            }
            mainMenu.MenuString = "";
        }
        elseif (mainMenu.MenuString.ToUppercase == "SPEED")
        {
            if (game.showCoordinates == false)
            {
                game.showCoordinates = true;
            }
            else
            {
                game.showCoordinates = false;
            }
            mainMenu.MenuString = "";
        }
        elseif (mainMenu.MenuString == "\027")
        {
            BackToGame;
        }
        elseif ((mainMenu.MenuString == "\013") || (mainMenu.MenuString     == " "))
        {
            MainMenuCommand(mainMenu.SelectedItem);
        }
        // Menu movement has yet to be implemented.
    }
}

I would, of course, need help from anyone who knows some C and/or C++.

Edit: Could some mod split this post from this thread, please?

Hmmm, you shouldnt post C like code if you didnt compile it yet and see if it work and I think isnt good to have the code here in the threads. Probably attaching the files or if possible creating a new project under sourceforge, perhaps?

Anyway well, you should use twinEngine to start doing the LBA RE. Why? Because you have lots of things already done that is equal and have the same meaning/subject if you get what Im trying to say.

This part your tryng to reverse is already done by twinEngine and its almost the same like LBA1, so you should see that code. This is the file that have this informations comportementMenu.c

And better isnt to make a new engine but use twinEngine for LBA2 aswell
raw:
Hmmm, you shouldn't post C like code if you didn't compile it yet and see if it work :) and I think isn't good to have the code here in the threads. Probably attaching the files or if possible creating a new project under sourceforge, perhaps?

Anyway well, you should use twinEngine to start doing the LBA RE. Why? Because you have lots of things already done that is equal and have the same meaning/subject if you get what I'm trying to say.

This part your tryng to reverse is already done by twinEngine and its almost the same like LBA1, so you should see that code. This is the file that have this informations [comportementMenu.c](http://cvs.sourceforge.net/viewcvs.py/twin-e/twin-e/src/comportementMenu.c?rev=1.13&view=auto)

... And better isn't to make a new engine but use twinEngine for LBA2 aswell ;)

cooked:
- alexfont: OK, thanks for the tip. I think Ill go from TwinEngine and just start modifying it from LBA 1 to LBA 2.

So first, I have to change some things in that menu:

  1. Add the cheat codes;
  2. Make the menu flexible, since in LBA 2, when you call the main menu from in-game, it has 6 buttons while when you start the executable file, it has 4 buttons; 5 buttons if theres a CURRENT.LBA file in the SAVE folder;
  3. Modify the borders to be rounder;
  4. Add the code to display the demo screenshots if the SCRSHOT.HQR is present and/or the #IFDEF DEMO is set;
  5. Make the CURRENT.LBA image be displayed when the CURRENT.LBA file is found in the SAVE folder;
  6. Modify the background image to be loaded from SCREEN.HQR instead of RESS.HQR as it is in LBA 1.

Anyway, Ill put into it the LBA 2 models code since I have it, thanks to yaz0r.

And yes, I agree with you. With just modifying TwinEngine from LBA 1 to LBA 2 we can have bigger compatibility between the two and also have the possibility to make some modification tutorials in the future, which will work with both.

Edit: I just submitted the registration for this project in SourceForge now, but I have to wait 2 days for it to be accepted (or possibly rejected) though

And in the end, thanks for splitting the thread.
Many good lucks to you.
raw: Many good lucks to you.

cooked: with this engine would you also be able make a isometric LBA2 outside?
raw: with this engine would you also be able make a isometric LBA2 outside?

cooked:
- Dan2552: You can already make an isometric LBA 2 outside - just convert the outside Brick Layout Libraries from LBA 1 to LBA 2, make some new bricks, layouts and libraries (mainly for Emerald Moon, Zeelich and Zeelich Undergas) and then make the grids using Zinks LBArchitect.

But yes, with this LBA 2 engine, it would be done much more easily than with LBArchitect + LBA Brick Layout Editor, since we will certainly learn many new things from it.
raw:
\- Dan2552: You can already make an isometric LBA 2 outside - just convert the outside Brick Layout Libraries from LBA 1 to LBA 2, make some new bricks, layouts and libraries (mainly for Emerald Moon, Zeelich and Zeelich Undergas) and then make the grids using Zink's LBArchitect.

But yes, with this LBA 2 engine, it would be done much more easily than with LBArchitect + LBA Brick Layout Editor, since we will certainly learn many new things from it.

cooked:
Symbian is an Operating System for mobile phones. For example, Nokia use it in his last models. Im now only trying for Nokia Serie60 phones because I have one (6630), anyway I didnt manage to get it to work yet because I need to change things in the project code including the mainSDL.c file that make possible run the game with the SDL interface. Anyway I already fix somethings, I just need time for it, thing that I dont have much this days :(.
But if I didnt manage to make it Ill ask help with some Symbian Gurus, it will be really great to have LBA on the mobile. I have now Monkey Island and the Day Of The Tentacle thought ScummVM and its great ;). Also the great Another World game, who doesnt remember this classic

cooked:
Hey Seb,
You can make the website but I think you shoudnt loose your time seting up a forum. Ill personaly not using it and for the bug tracking system we have SF, thats why well also have the project there.
For now the website only need to display only one page that shows we start planning it.

The project stills in the very beginning and I only did the SDL functions, HQR management and the only thing is showed are the first screens related with the right version of the game used.

About the ports: Ill not post the CVS till I manage to decode the LBA2 main menu (still figure out the things), and about the ports, Ill only release a win version because people who use linux could easly get the source and compile it. About the MAC, only if someone have one and can compile it for us.
Anyway this is really, but really early to talk about this stuffs. Well not see a release for months because this isnt easy and need time to learn and do the things, thing I dont have much now-a-days.

Ok, PM if you want.
raw:
Hey Seb,
You can make the website but I think you shoudn't loose your time seting up a forum. I'll personaly not using it and for the bug tracking system we have SF, thats why we'll also have the project there.
For now the website only need to display only one page that shows we start planning it.

The project stills in the very beginning and I only did the SDL functions, HQR management and the only thing is showed are the first screens related with the right version of the game used.

About the ports: I'll not post the CVS till I manage to decode the LBA2 main menu (still figure out the things), and about the ports, I'll only release a win version because people who use linux could easly get the source and compile it. About the MAC, only if someone have one and can compile it for us.
Anyway this is really, but really early to talk about this stuffs. We'll not see a release for months because this isn't easy and need time to learn and do the things, thing I don't have much now-a-days.

Ok, PM if you want.
